title: Lucid Interval After Head Trauma - Epidural Hematoma
chief_complaint: 25-year-old male hit head in motor vehicle accident. Initially conscious, then became confused, now unresponsive with right pupil dilated. Lucid interval of 2 hours.

physical_exam.neuro: GCS 6 (E1 V2 M3), right pupil fixed and dilated, left hemiparesis, Cushing triad (HTN, bradycardia, irregular breathing)

hidden_data.ct_head: Biconvex (lens-shaped) hyperdensity right temporoparietal region, midline shift 12mm left, uncal herniation

correct_diagnosis: Acute Epidural Hematoma with Uncal Herniation (MMA Rupture)

patient_responses.accident: Car accident, he hit his head on the windshield
patient_responses.lucid: He was talking after the accident, then suddenly deteriorated

feedback.correct_message: Excellent! EDH with herniation - neurosurgical emergency.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is an epidural hematoma. The lucid interval + CT findings are classic.
feedback.attending_note: EDH with herniation: Emergency craniotomy and evacuation. Mannitol 1g/kg IV. Hyperventilation to PaCO2 30-35. Elevate head of bed 30°. Excellent prognosis if evacuated promptly before irreversible brainstem damage.
